fix: PreToolUse deny guard (spawn-guard pattern, ADR-0008): a *_progress.json write outside .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/ is denied with a redirect naming both homes; default OFF, fail-safe, one invocation record per firing, toggle optional in hooks-config.schema.json. Commit e7f4cd5 (10 files). code-standards + package-qa PASS; 15-check payload simulation.
title: Progress-location deny guard: misfiled Progress Record writes are denied with a redirect
description: A PreToolUse guard in the spawn-guard pattern denies a *_progress.json write outside .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/, with a redirect reason naming the right home. Rules/decision split, hooks.config toggle, every firing invocation-logged, fail-safe on any error (a broken guard never blocks legitimate writes). Location only — schema and content stay the gates' job. End-to-end: a simulated misfiled write is denied with the redirect; a correct-home write passes untouched.
acceptance_criteria: Misfiled *_progress.json write denied with a reason naming .excn/progress/ or .excn/runtime/
Writes inside the two homes pass untouched; non-progress writes unaffected
Toggle in hooks.config.json; malformed config or payload fails safe (exit 0, no output)
Every firing logs one invocation record
Payload-simulation tests cover deny, pass, and fail-safe paths
Src-First: template hook source first, dogfood root mirrors; code-standards gate PASSes
